behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names all key paths (packages/app/src/testing/terminal.ts, terminal.tsx, actions.ts, fixtures.ts), the terminalAttr constant value 'data-pty-id', state fields (connected/rendered/settled), window structure __opencode_e2e.terminal, and helper names waitTerminalReady/runTerminal.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what needs to exist and what events to track, but doesn't dictate function signatures, internal helper names, or line-level edits.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests use flexible matching (Object.values to find probe function, case-insensitive method name matching for init/connect/render/settle/cleanup) and check behavior rather than exact names.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute TypeScript via node --experimental-strip-types, import the module, call functions, and inspect state; only integration tests do text checks.

test_not_tautological.reason: test_terminal_probe_tracks_state requires rendered='hello world' and settled=1 after calls; a stub returning defaults would fail these concrete assertions.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover probe state tracking, cleanup, noop-without-window, terminal.tsx integration, actions exports, and fixtures addInitScript setup — matching the 5 instruction requirements.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped under comment headers (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass, Integration) with tags like [pr_diff] fail_to_pass.

structured_data_schema.reason: Instruction documents the state shape: {connected: boolean, rendered: string, settled: number} and window.__opencode_e2e.terminal = {enabled: true, terminals: {}}.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names are consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction points to relevant files but doesn't leak specific buggy lines; the fix involves creating new code, not localizing a bug.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code task, not an agentmd-edits config task.
